name: Drunken Pioneers
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the green bell pepper and onion into strips.
2. Cook over high heat in a large skillet until vegetables just begin to brown.
3. Add broth, wine, basil, and rosemary; bring mixture to a boil.
4. Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes.
5. Dissolve flour in water, then add the mixture to the skillet.
6. Increase heat and boil until sauce has thickened.
7. Pour contents of the skillet over the wagon wheel pasta in a bowl and serve.
("Drunken Pioneers" = "Wagon Wheels in Wine Sauce")
Source: Lisa Clarke
Notes: This recipe was an experiment born out of a messy kitchen and a lack of meat in the freezer. It smelled very good while cooking, but was actually quite bland. The vegetables absorbed the flavor of the sauce, but the pasta didn't seem affected at all. Beef strips or a night in the fridge might help this dish.
